outcome_text: The organization's mission and relevant goals for AI technology are understood.
scoring_text: Knowledge of organizational mission alignment for AI technology, including understanding how AI systems relate to organizational strategic objectives, interpreting mission-relevant goals in terms of AI capabilities, and ensuring AI technology decisions align with organizational purpose.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of organizational mission alignment for AI technology, including understanding how AI systems relate to organizational strategic objectives, interpreting mission-relevant goals in terms of AI capabilities, and ensuring AI technology decisions align with organizational purpose.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in aligning data and AI program objectives to organizational strategy, translating business priorities into data and AI capability requirements with measurable outcomes.
comments: NIST AI RMF MP-1.4 (MAP) equal WIDAI DG domain. DG-S-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.814, cosine: 0.694). Secondary: AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.636), AI-T-018 (AI, cosine: 0.626).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 8.1/10 (raw 0.814). Shared vocabulary: [ai, objectives, organizational]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [align, alignment, capabilities, decisions, ensuring, goals] vs WIDAI terms [aligning, business, capability, data, measurable, outcomes] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.906 (P=0.907, R=0.905). Balanced P/R — the 35-word competency implication and 23-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.6%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.694 < STS 0.814 (gap -0.121). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning between [align, alignment, capabilities] and [aligning, business, capability] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.1%. Shared tokens: [ai, objectives, organizational]. NIST AI RMF-only: [align, alignment, capabilities, decisions, ensuring, goals]. WIDAI-only: [aligning, business, capability, data, measurable, outcomes].
